Q.description: The smallest quantity of antique bottles that must be manufactured

D.description: Volume of an antique bottle measured in milliliters

O.description: The smallest proportion of standard bottles compared to antique bottles.

J.description: Volume of a typical bottle measured in milliliters

A.description: The overall quantity of wine that is in milliliters and ready for use.

assumptions.description: The smallest quantity of antique bottles that must be manufactured must be non-negative.
The volume of an antique bottle measured in milliliters must be non-negative.
The smallest proportion of standard bottles compared to antique bottles must be non-negative.
The volume of a typical bottle measured in milliliters must be non-negative.
The overall quantity of wine that is in milliliters and ready for use must be non-negative.

z.description: The quantity of standard bottles needed for production

g.description: The quantity of old bottles to manufacture

constraints.description: The quantity of standard bottles must be a minimum of O times the quantity of vintage bottles.
A minimum of Q bottles with a vintage must be manufactured.
The combined quantity of vine used in vintage and regular bottles should not surpass A milliliters.
The number of regular bottles produced must be non-negative.
The number of vintage bottles produced must be non-negative.

objective.description: Optimize the overall quantity of bottles manufactured.
